stimulus: The Harlem Renaissance saw the founding of numerous Black-owned publishing ventures, small presses that printed essays, poetry, and political tracts. Among the ventures' founders ______blank Jessie Redmon Fauset, literary editor of The Crisis magazine.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. The convention being tested here is subject-verb agreement. The singular verb "was" agrees in number with the singular subject "Jessie Redmon Fauset."

Choice A is incorrect because the plural verb "are" doesn't agree in number with the singular subject "Jessie Redmon Fauset." Choice B is incorrect because the plural verb "have been" doesn't agree in number with the singular subject "Jessie Redmon Fauset." Choice D is incorrect because the plural verb "were" doesn't agree in number with the singular subject "Jessie Redmon Fauset."
